hook: You already know how to analyze a single random variable — its distribution, its mean, its variance. But most real-world problems involve two or more quantities that are tangled together: how does the height of a person relate to their weight, or how does studying time connect to exam score? This unit gives you the tools to model and measure those relationships precisely.
concept: The unifying idea is that two random variables together carry more information than either does alone, and understanding their *joint distribution* lets you capture dependence, compute conditional behavior, and quantify how strongly variables move together — formally captured by the concept of the *joint probability distribution* and its derived quantities.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to represent the simultaneous behavior of two discrete random variables using a joint probability mass function and verify it satisfies the probability axioms.
Master how to extract the distribution of one variable from a joint distribution, and how to update that distribution given knowledge about the other variable using conditional distributions.
Apply covariance and the correlation coefficient $\rho = \frac{\text{Cov}(X,Y)}{\sigma_X \sigma_Y}$ to measure the direction and strength of the linear relationship between two random variables.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Joint Continuous Distributions via Double Integrals
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to work with joint probability density functions for continuous random variables, computing probabilities and expectations over two-dimensional regions using double integrals.
